source.url.reason: Fragment URL slug ends in '-testing-laboratories' which is a dead path. Confirmed live URL is '/nationally-recognized-testing-laboratory-program' (singular 'laboratory', no trailing 's').

standards_or_laws[UL 62368-1 Ed. 3].note: Ed. 3 (Dec 2019, ANSI-approved Oct 2021) remains the current NRTL listing standard for US market purposes. However, UL/CSA 62368-1 4th Edition was published July 31 2025. Row should note that Ed. 4 exists and NRTLs may begin accepting it for new listings; the transition period is underway. No mandatory deadline yet for US market but this should be flagged.

standards_or_laws[UL 60335-2-41].note: UL 60335-2-41 (Pumps) flag is appropriate. UL research confirms that CDU cooling assemblies with refrigerant/vapor-compression sub-systems are evaluated under UL/CAN/IEC 60335-2-40 (Heat Pumps, Air-Conditioners and Dehumidifiers), not UL 60335-2-41. Non-refrigerant pump-only sub-assemblies may fall under 60335-2-41 but this is uncommon for CDU designs. Flag retained; recommend testing lab confirm scope.

note: Core content accurate. NRTL requirement under 29 CFR 1910.7 and NEC 110.3(B) is correctly characterised as mandatory. Gap between Chinese GB/CCC and US NRTL is accurate — no MRA exists. GB/T 42315-2023 adoption of IEC 62368-1 confirmed (published 2023). CCC mandatory status for CDU export products remains unverified as stated. HBSE framework description accurate. One source URL fix required (dead slug). UL 62368-1 Ed. 4 (July 2025) is a new development not in the fragment — flag for editorial review.

source.url.reason: URL slug 'datacom-equipment' does not match confirmed live ASHRAE bookstore path. The datacom-series page exists and lists the TC 9.9 publications. Note: from 2024, ASHRAE moved liquid cooling guidelines to the Datacom Encyclopedia at https://datacom.ashrae.org — the bookstore URL is still live but points to legacy print editions. Updated source to the confirmed live bookstore URL; best canonical reference going forward is https://datacom.ashrae.org.

target_requirement.summary.note: ASHRAE TC 9.9 Liquid Cooling Guidelines book has been superseded by the ASHRAE TC 9.9 Datacom Encyclopedia (launched 2024, https://datacom.ashrae.org), which consolidates all prior TC 9.9 publications. Substance unchanged; reference should acknowledge the encyclopedia transition.

standards_or_laws[ASME BPVC Section VIII.note: ASME BPVC Sec VIII Div 1 confirmed to apply to vessels with internal/external pressure exceeding 15 psig. The 6-inch inside-diameter threshold is an exclusion (vessels <6 in i.d. are exempt). Row correctly describes both thresholds. The 2025 edition of BPVC is the most current (confirmed on asme.org).

note: Three-part row (ASHRAE TC 9.9, NEC, ASME BPVC) is substantively accurate. ASHRAE TC 9.9 as voluntary/industry-mandatory (not law) is correctly described. NEC adoption by all 50 states via mandatory adoption by reference is accurate. ASME BPVC stamping mandatory under most US state PV laws is accurate. Gap description (no CN MRA for ASME or NEC) is accurate. Source URL slug needs correction. ASHRAE content migration to Datacom Encyclopedia (2024) should be noted.

standards_or_laws[NFPA 75].old: NFPA 75 (2020 edition — current; 2025 edition published, adoption varies by jurisdiction)
standards_or_laws[NFPA 75].new: NFPA 75 (2024 edition — current; adoption by jurisdiction varies; 2020 edition in force in some jurisdictions still adopting)
standards_or_laws[NFPA 75].reason: NFPA 75 2024 edition was published and is the current edition. There is NO 2025 edition. The 2020 edition is no longer the latest. Row must be corrected in all three language fields. The 2024 edition adds Chapter 8 requirements specifically for ITE immersion cooling equipment, including a flash-point requirement (Class IIIB liquid with closed-cup flash point >= 135 C / 275 F) and off-gas detection. This is directly relevant to the row topic.

standards_or_laws[NFPA 30].note: NFPA 30 2024 edition is the current edition (confirmed). Row cites NFPA 30 without edition; should reference 2024 edition.

note: Source URL https://www.nfpa.org/codes-and-standards/nfpa-75-standard-development/75 is live and official. NFPA 76 2020 is still the current edition (confirmed — 2023 edition cycle is still in development as of 2026). NFPA 30 current edition is 2024. The critical error is calling '2020 edition current' and referencing a nonexistent '2025 edition' — the 2024 edition is current and adds immersion cooling Chapter 8 provisions that should be cited in this row. Mandatory status description for NFPA 75/76 (adopted by jurisdiction) is accurate. Gap description (CN GB 50174 lacks NFPA 75 flash-point classification linkage) is accurate and substantiated by the 2024 edition's new Chapter 8 requirements.

source.url.reason: Fragment URL path 'epa.gov/tsca-reporting/...' is incorrect/dead. Confirmed live official EPA page is at 'epa.gov/assessing-and-managing-chemicals-under-tsca/tsca-section-8a7-reporting-and-recordkeeping'.

target_requirement.summary[TSCA 8a7 dead.old: reporting period opened 2024; data due 2025 for most filers
target_requirement.summary[TSCA 8a7 dead.new: reporting period has been extended multiple times; as of April 2026 EPA finalised that the submission period start is 60 days after EPA issues a forthcoming substantive revision to the rule, with January 31 2027 as the backstop date. Most filers are currently expected to submit by October 13 2026 under the May 2025 interim final rule (but the April 2026 amendment may alter this); small businesses importing PFAS in articles have a later deadline. Verify current deadline at the EPA source URL before relying.
target_requirement.summary[TSCA 8a7 dead.reason: The 'data due 2025' statement is materially outdated. The reporting deadline has been extended twice (Sep 2024 and May 2025) and further amended by a final rule published April 2026. Current deadline for most filers under May 2025 IFR is Oct 13 2026; April 2026 rule introduces a further backstop of Jan 31 2027.

target_requirement.summary[3M exit].old: 3M exiting PFAS production by end of 2025
target_requirement.summary[3M exit].new: 3M exited PFAS production: last Novec order date was March 31 2025, manufacturing lines shut down by end of 2025. As of 2026, Novec 7100, 7000, 649, and Fluorinert FC-72 are discontinued. Supply chain for two-phase immersion cooling using 3M fluids is disrupted; operators must qualify alternative fluids.
target_requirement.summary[3M exit].reason: 3M exit is confirmed and complete as of end-2025. The fragment correctly flags this as a risk but understates that the supply disruption has materialized — 3M Novec is gone as of early 2026, not merely pending.

note: TSCA 8(a)(7) regulation confirmed in 40 CFR Part 705 (effective 2023, rule finalized Oct 11 2023). TSCA Section 6 risk evaluation authority is correctly described. State PFAS law flags are appropriate and must remain — state applicability to industrial cooling fluids varies widely and has not been confirmed for this product category. China MEE Order No. 12 (2020) characterisation as not imposing PFAS-specific cooling fluid restrictions is accurate. Two fixes needed: (1) dead source URL, (2) deadline date materially outdated. Both en/zh/zht language fields must be updated.

standards_or_laws[UL 62368-1 source.url].reason: The fragment URL 'ul.com/resources/ul-62368-1-standard' is a resource/article page, not the canonical standards catalog entry. The UL Standards & Engagement catalog page at standardscatalog.ul.com is the correct authoritative URL for the standard itself. Note: UL/CSA 62368-1 4th edition was published July 31 2025; transition period is underway. The 3rd edition (_3 suffix) remains the current NRTL listing standard for most purposes in the US market.

standards_or_laws[OSHA NRTL source.url].note: URL 'osha.gov/nationally-recognized-testing-laboratory-program' confirmed live and official. Correct.

note: Main source URL (OSHA NRTL page) confirmed live and official. UL 62368-1 as primary NRTL listing standard for IT/DC equipment is accurate. NEC Article 110.3(B) listing requirement confirmed. 29 CFR 1910.7 as NRTL definition regulation confirmed. NFPA 70 URL confirmed live. Gap description (CCC/GB not accepted by US AHJs; no MRA; 3-9 month timeline) is accurate and fair. mandatory_status correctly distinguishes NEC mandatory from CCC mandatory-only-for-catalogue products. UL standards catalog URL should replace the resource article URL. No material content errors.

source.url.reason: The 'advanced-cooling-solutions' sub-project URL is stale — OCP consolidated the ACS and ACF sub-projects under the Cooling Environments project (https://www.opencompute.org/projects/cooling-environments). The old URL does not resolve to an active project page. The Cooling Environments project is the current home for CDU, immersion, cold-plate, and facility liquid cooling specifications.

standards_or_laws[OCP source.url].reason: Same consolidation as above. OCP advanced cooling specifications (cold plate, CDU, immersion) now live under Cooling Environments. The ACS Liquid Cooling Cold Plate Requirements and OCP Modular TCS (Aug 2025) are published under this project.

standards_or_laws[ASHRAE TC 9.9 source.u.reason: ASHRAE TC 9.9 liquid cooling content migrated from the Datacom book series to the ASHRAE TC 9.9 Datacom Encyclopedia (https://datacom.ashrae.org) launched in 2024. The bookstore/datacom-series page still exists for legacy print sales but the living reference is the encyclopedia. The note about '5th Ed.' (2021) remains correct for the thermal guidelines document; the encyclopedia now consolidates this.

standards_or_laws[ASHRAE TC 9.9 edition.new: 5th edition release date confirmed: 2021. The 2024 TC 9.9 Datacom Encyclopedia supersedes individual books. A September 2024 Technical Bulletin on liquid cooling resilience was published.
standards_or_laws[ASHRAE TC 9.9 edition.reason: 5th edition date (2021) is confirmed. The unverified flag on the 5th edition release date can be resolved; the conformance testing process note remains appropriately flagged.

note: NDAA FY2019 Section 889 confirmed (Pub. L. 115-232; FAR 52.204-25 URL https://www.acquisition.gov/far/52.204-25 confirmed live and correct). Named entities (Huawei, ZTE, Hikvision, Dahua, Hytera) confirmed accurate. EO 13873 / ICTS Rule (15 CFR Part 7) characterisation as for cooling hardware is appropriate — confirm with counsel. OCP URL is stale and must be updated to Cooling Environments project. ASHRAE Datacom Series URL should point to the encyclopedia. Mandatory status correctly labels OCP as de-facto, NDAA as mandatory for federal contractors, ASHRAE as not law. Gap description (dual barrier: OCP qualification + NDAA/ICTS scrutiny) is accurate.
